title: DSL/config editor with schema validation and live error squiggles
business_context: YAML config editor for DevOps: collaborate on Kubernetes manifests; live schema check + lint markers. Scale: 5k orgs.
architecture_reasoning: 1. PG for configs + versions. Server-side validator.

database_design.schema: CREATE TABLE configs(id BIGSERIAL PK,project_id BIGINT,name TEXT,body TEXT,version INT DEFAULT 1,schema_id BIGINT);\nCREATE TABLE config_schemas(id BIGSERIAL PK,name TEXT,schema_json JSONB);

scaling_considerations: 10k-line doc: ajv compile cached per schema.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Junior: lint on every keystroke. Server CPU melt.

title: Inline AI-suggested replies in customer chat
business_context: Agent inbox: AI suggests 3 reply options; agent picks/edits. Scale: 500 agents.

database_design.schema: CREATE TABLE ai_suggest_log(id BIGSERIAL PK,chat_id BIGINT,agent_id BIGINT,suggestions JSONB,picked_idx INT,edited_diff TEXT,at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now());

race_conditions_addressed: Agent disconnect: socket.emit goes to that socket only.
scaling_considerations: LLM cost: cache by context hash.

title: Server uptime monitor with multi-region probe
business_context: Status page: probes from 5 regions; if 3+ red, mark service down. Scale: 1k services.

database_design.schema: const ProbeSchema=new Schema({serviceId:String,region:String,ok:Boolean,latencyMs:Number,ts:Date},{timeseries:{timeField:'ts',metaField:'serviceId'}});\nconst ServiceStatusSchema=new Schema({_id:String,name:String,status:String,maintenance:Boolean,updatedAt:Date});

title: Co-op puzzle with shared inventory
business_context: 2-player puzzle: shared inventory; both can grab; coordinate. Scale: 5k rooms.

database_design.schema: const PuzzleRoomSchema=new Schema({_id:String,players:[String],inventory:[{itemId:String,holderId:String}],solved:Boolean,version:Number});

race_conditions_addressed: Disconnect: holder remains; idle timer could release (omitted).
what_breaks_naive_approach: Junior: client checks. Both think they grabbed.

title: Reverse auction (suppliers compete to lowest)
business_context: Procurement: buyer posts RFQ; suppliers bid lowest. Scale: 1k RFQs/day.

database_design.schema: CREATE TABLE rfqs(id BIGSERIAL PK,buyer_id BIGINT,description TEXT,deadline TIMESTAMPTZ,current_low_cents BIGINT,current_supplier BIGINT,status TEXT);\nCREATE TABLE rfq_bids(id BIGSERIAL PK,rfq_id BIGINT,supplier_id BIGINT,amount_cents BIGINT,at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now());

race_conditions_addressed: Snipe: auto-extend if bid in last 30s (omitted).
what_breaks_naive_approach: Junior: client price calc. Below-cost validation bypassed.

title: Live community speaker queue (stage app)
business_context: Twitter Spaces-style: listeners raise hands; moderators promote to speaker. Scale: 1k spaces.

database_design.schema: const SpaceSchema=new Schema({_id:String,hostIds:[String],speakerIds:[String],listenerCount:Number,handQueue:[{userId:String,at:Date}],recording:Boolean});

title: Slide deck collaborative annotation during presentation
business_context: Live presentation: presenter shares slides; viewers annotate; presenter pins key questions. Scale: 1k events.

database_design.schema: CREATE TABLE decks(id BIGSERIAL PK,presenter_id BIGINT,current_slide INT,total_slides INT);\nCREATE TABLE slide_annotations(id BIGSERIAL PK,deck_id BIGINT,slide INT,author_id BIGINT,text TEXT,pinned BOOLEAN DEFAULT false,created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now());\nCREATE TABLE slide_reactions(deck_id BIGINT,slide INT,user_id BIGINT,emoji TEXT,at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),PRIMARY KEY(deck_id,slide,user_id,emoji));

title: App lifecycle events: install/uninstall fan-out for analytics
business_context: Mobile app install tracking; events stream to dashboards in real time. Scale: 100k events/min.
architecture_reasoning: 1. PG for raw + counts. Mongo for cohort metadata.

database_design.schema: CREATE TABLE app_events(id BIGSERIAL PK,device_id TEXT,event_type TEXT,app_version TEXT,ts TIMESTAMPTZ,UNIQUE(device_id,event_type,ts));\nCREATE TABLE event_hourly(hour TIMESTAMPTZ,event_type TEXT,count BIGINT,PRIMARY KEY(hour,event_type));

race_conditions_addressed: Late event: hourly bucket might recompute.

title: Sports broadcast: live stats + commentator tools
business_context: Sports app: live score, possession, shot stats. Stats team enters; viewers see. Scale: 200 matches simul.

database_design.schema: CREATE TABLE matches(id BIGSERIAL PK,team_a BIGINT,team_b BIGINT,score_a INT DEFAULT 0,score_b INT DEFAULT 0,status TEXT);\nCREATE TABLE match_stat_events(id BIGSERIAL PK,match_id BIGINT,kind TEXT,team_id BIGINT,player_id BIGINT,at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),corrected BOOLEAN DEFAULT false);

race_conditions_addressed: Correction race: WHERE NOT corrected guard.
Concurrent entry: per-event row, no conflict.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Junior: score = client increment. Drift on retry.

title: Class breakout rooms with auto-distribute
business_context: Teacher breaks class into N rooms; auto-distributes students. Recall to main. Scale: 500 classes.

database_design.schema: CREATE TABLE breakouts(id BIGSERIAL PK,class_id BIGINT,room_no INT,sfu_node TEXT);\nCREATE TABLE breakout_assignments(breakout_id BIGINT,student_id BIGINT,PRIMARY KEY(breakout_id,student_id));

race_conditions_addressed: Add room mid-session: separate distribute call.

title: Multi-stakeholder change request approval
business_context: ITSM change request: 3 approvers (peer, manager, security); each signs. Scale: 50k orgs.
architecture_reasoning: 1. PG for state machine. Mongo for comments.

database_design.schema: CREATE TABLE change_requests(id BIGSERIAL PK,title TEXT,status TEXT,current_approver_role TEXT,sla_at TIMESTAMPTZ);\nCREATE TABLE cr_approvals(cr_id BIGINT,role TEXT,approver_id BIGINT,decision TEXT,at TIMESTAMPTZ,PRIMARY KEY(cr_id,role));\nconst CrCommentSchema=new Schema({crId:String,authorId:String,body:String,at:Date});

what_breaks_naive_approach: Junior: parallel approvals. Manager approves before peer = bypass.

title: Subscription proration calc with live preview
business_context: User changes plan mid-cycle; preview proration before commit. Scale: 1k changes/hour.
architecture_reasoning: 1. PG for subscriptions + plan history. Preview is pure calc.

database_design.schema: CREATE TABLE subscriptions(id BIGSERIAL PK,user_id BIGINT,plan_id BIGINT,starts_at TIMESTAMPTZ,renews_at TIMESTAMPTZ,price_cents BIGINT);\nCREATE TABLE plan_changes(id BIGSERIAL PK,sub_id BIGINT,from_plan BIGINT,to_plan BIGINT,prorated_credit_cents BIGINT,prorated_charge_cents BIGINT,at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now());

title: Knowledge base wiki with section-level edit locks
business_context: Internal wiki: section locks; multi-author. Scale: 100k pages.
architecture_reasoning: 1. Mongo for pages + sections. Redis for locks.

database_design.schema: const WikiPageSchema=new Schema({_id:String,title:String,sections:[{_id:String,heading:String,body:String,version:Number}],updatedAt:Date});\nconst PageRevSchema=new Schema({_id:String,pageId:String,sectionId:String,body:String,authorId:String,at:Date});

race_conditions_addressed: Lock leak: disconnect handler iterates held; TTL on Redis hash.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Junior: page-level lock. Slow editor blocks all others.

title: Internal messaging with @here vs @everyone semantics
business_context: Workspace channels: @here pings online only; @everyone all. Scale: 100k channels.
architecture_reasoning: 1. PG for messages + audit. Redis for presence.

database_design.schema: -- as prior chat schemas.
database_design.queries: On @here: filter members by online state in Redis.

race_conditions_addressed: Presence flicker: server snapshot at send time.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Junior: trust client which is @here. Members list leaked.

title: Database query observability with slow-query live tail
business_context: DBA app: live tail of pg_stat_statements; flag queries > 1s. Scale: 100 DBs.
architecture_reasoning: 1. Worker polls pg_stat_statements per DB; streams to dashboard.

database_design.schema: CREATE TABLE slow_queries(id BIGSERIAL PK,db_id BIGINT,query_hash TEXT,query_redacted TEXT,duration_ms NUMERIC,calls BIGINT,at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now());\nCREATE INDEX ON slow_queries(db_id,at DESC);

what_breaks_naive_approach: Junior: log raw queries. Sensitive data leak.

title: Word race typing test with live WPM and ranks
business_context: Typeracer: typing race; live WPM. Scale: 1k races.

database_design.schema: const RaceSchema=new Schema({_id:String,text:String,players:[{userId:String,charsTyped:Number,wpm:Number,finishedAt:Date,joinedAt:Date}],status:String,startedAt:Date});

race_conditions_addressed: Tie at finish: finishedAt by server time.
Paste cheat: client compares timestamps of chars; server flags rate > 20 chars/sec.

title: Sports collectible auction with provenance chain
business_context: Trading cards: bids + provenance chain stored. Scale: 5k auctions/day.
architecture_reasoning: 1. PG for auctions + chain entries with hash.

database_design.schema: CREATE TABLE collectibles(id BIGSERIAL PK,description TEXT);\nCREATE TABLE provenance(id BIGSERIAL PK,collectible_id BIGINT,owner_id BIGINT,acquired_at TIMESTAMPTZ,prev_hash BYTEA,row_hash BYTEA);

frontend_solution: // uses sock-005 patterns

title: Pair programming presence with shared editor focus
business_context: Pair programming: 2 devs see each other's editor focus + cursor. Scale: 1k pairs.
architecture_reasoning: 1. PG for session + driver. Redis for ephemeral cursor.

database_design.schema: CREATE TABLE pair_sessions(id BIGSERIAL PK,dev_a BIGINT,dev_b BIGINT,driver_id BIGINT,started_at TIMESTAMPTZ,switched_at TIMESTAMPTZ);

race_conditions_addressed: Disconnect: idle timer reassigns or pauses.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Junior: client decides driver. Both think they are.

title: Receipt scanning with per-line-item annotation
business_context: Expense management: OCR scans receipt; user/manager annotate per line. Scale: 100k scans/day.
architecture_reasoning: 1. PG for expenses. Mongo for receipt data + lines.

database_design.schema: CREATE TABLE expenses(id BIGSERIAL PK,user_id BIGINT,status TEXT,total_cents BIGINT);\nconst ReceiptSchema=new Schema({_id:String,expenseId:String,photoKey:String,lines:[{idx:Number,description:String,qty:Number,priceCents:Number,confidence:Number,corrected:Boolean,version:Number}]});

title: Service alert routing to on-call rotation
business_context: Alerts route to current on-call; escalation if no ack in 5min. Scale: 50 services.

database_design.schema: CREATE TABLE rotations(id BIGSERIAL PK,service_id BIGINT,user_id BIGINT,starts_at TIMESTAMPTZ,ends_at TIMESTAMPTZ);\nCREATE TABLE alerts(id BIGSERIAL PK,service_id BIGINT,title TEXT,severity TEXT,fired_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),acked_at TIMESTAMPTZ,acked_by BIGINT,escalation_level INT DEFAULT 0);

race_conditions_addressed: Schedule swap mid: server queries current at fire time.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Junior: hardcode on-call. Schedule changes ignored.

title: Smart parking with per-spot availability
business_context: Smart parking lot: sensors per spot; app shows availability map. Scale: 10k lots.

database_design.schema: const SpotSchema=new Schema({_id:String,lotId:String,occupied:Boolean,reservedBy:String,reservationExpiresAt:Date,updatedAt:Date});\nSpotSchema.index({lotId:1});

race_conditions_addressed: Sensor stuck: ignore for reserve; flag for ops.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Junior: client checks. Two cars race for spot.

title: Call queue with skill-based routing
business_context: Inbound calls routed to agent with right skill. Scale: 200 agents.

database_design.schema: CREATE TABLE call_queue(id BIGSERIAL PK,phone TEXT,skill TEXT,queued_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),assigned_agent BIGINT,status TEXT);\nCREATE TABLE agents(id BIGSERIAL PK,skills TEXT[],status TEXT);

title: Recipe rating form with photo attachments
business_context: Cookbook app: users rate + photo recipes. Scale: 1M users.

database_design.schema: const ReviewSchema=new Schema({_id:String,recipeId:String,userId:String,stars:Number,body:String,photoKeys:[String],helpfulBy:[String],createdAt:Date});\nReviewSchema.index({recipeId:1,createdAt:-1});

title: Long polling fallback for low-tier networks
business_context: Some users on networks blocking WebSocket; fall back to long-polling cleanly. Scale: 5% of traffic.
architecture_reasoning: 1. Socket.IO built-in supports polling fallback; tune client config.

database_design.schema: // same

backend_solution: // Server config:\nimport {Server} from 'socket.io';\nconst io=new Server(httpServer,{transports:['websocket','polling'],allowEIO3:true,pingInterval:25000,pingTimeout:60000});
frontend_solution: import {io} from 'socket.io-client';\nexport function getResilientSocket(token:string){return io('/',{auth:{token},transports:['websocket','polling'],reconnection:true,reconnectionDelayMax:5000});}
race_conditions_addressed: Upgrade fail: client falls back automatically.
scaling_considerations: Polling has higher server cost; cap per node.

title: Capstone: codebase issue+PR+chat unified surface
business_context: Linear+GitHub-style: issues, PRs, chat threads unified per project. Scale: 50k projects.
architecture_reasoning: 1. PG for issues + PRs. Mongo for threads + chat. Unified event stream via outbox (sock-143).
race_conditions_addressed: Cross-tenant: per-room project key + perm middleware.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Junior: separate sockets per entity. Reconnect storms. Permission inconsistency.
